description: [Rama, further enquires Viswamitra narrates the birth of Tataka, her marriage, curse, etc., and convinces Rama to slay her.]

verses.dev: अथ तस्याप्रमेयस्य मुनेर्वचनमुत्तमम्।श्रुत्वा पुरुषशार्दूल: प्रत्युवाच शुभां गिरम्
verses.trans: On hearing the excellent words of Viswamitra, a sage with unimaginable faculty. Rama, the tiger among men (Rama) replied in a, gentle voice:

words.m: having super human faculty beyond comprehension

verses.dev: अल्पवीर्या यदा यक्षा श्श्रूयन्ते मुनिपुङ्गव।कथन्नागसहस्रस्य धारयत्यबला बलम्
verses.trans: "O best of ascetics, I have heard the yakshas possess little prowess. How can a woman who is by gender weak possess the strength of a thousand elephants?

verses.dev: विश्वामित्रोऽब्रवीद्वाक्यं शृण येन बलोत्तरा। वरदानकृतं वीर्यं धारयत्यबला बलम्
verses.trans: Viswamitra said, "Listen, how she acquired the prowess and strength by virtue of a boon she received.

verses.dev: पूर्वमासीन्महायक्षस्सुकेतुर्नाम वीर्यवान्।अनपत्यश्शुभाचारस्स च तेपे महत्तप:
verses.trans: "In the past there was a powerful and great yaksha by name Suketu. He had no children. So he performed an intense penance following virtuous practices.

verses.dev: पितामहस्तु सुप्रीतस्तस्य यक्षपते स्तदा।कन्यारत्नं ददौ राम ताटकां नाम नामत:
verses.trans: Rama the grandsire Brahma was highly gratified and granted to the king of yakshas, the gem of a daughter by name known asTataka.

verses.dev: ददौ नागसहस्रस्य बलं चास्या: पितामह:।नत्वेव पुत्रं यक्षाय ददौ ब्रह्मा महायशा:
verses.trans: The illustrious grandsire Brahma granted her the strength of a thousand elephants, but did not bestow a son on the yaksha.

verses.dev: तां तु जातां विवर्धन्तीं रूपयौवनशालिनीम्।झर्झपुत्राय सुन्दाय ददौ भार्यां यशस्विनीम्
verses.trans: As she grew up, glowing with youth and beauty she was given in marriage to Sunda, son of Jharjha.

words.m: shining with youthful charm and beauty

verses.dev: कस्यचित्त्वथ कालस्य यक्षी पुत्रमजायत।मारीचं नाम दुर्धर्षं यश्शापाद्राक्षसोऽभवत्
verses.trans: After some time that yakshini gave birth to an unassailable son by name Maricha. Because of a curse, he became a rakshasa (though born of yaksha parentage).

verses.dev: सुन्दे तु निहते राम सागस्त्यमृषिसत्तमम्।ताटका सह पुत्रेण प्रधर्षयितुमिच्छति
verses.trans: O Rama when Sunda died Tataka along with her son wanted to attack Agastya, the best of sages.

verses.dev: भक्षार्थं जातसंरम्भा गर्जन्ती साभ्यधावत।0आपतन्तीं तु तां दृष्ट्वा अगस्त्यो भगवानृषि:राक्षसत्वं भजस्वेति मारीचं व्याजहार स:। 1
verses.trans: Roaring, she rushed with excitement to devour him. The venerable rishi Agastya saw her approaching him and cursed Maricha to assume form of a demoness.

verses.dev: अगस्त्य: परमक्रुद्धस्ताटकामपि शप्तवान्पुरुषादी महायक्षी विरूपा विकृतानना।इदं रूपं विहायाथ दारुणं रूपमस्तु ते
verses.trans: Mighty angry, Agastya cursed Tataka saying, 'Abandon this form of a great yakshini and assume the terrible figure of a rakshasi, a cannibal with distorted appearance and a hideous countenance'.

words.m: let it be a terrible form.

verses.dev: अगस्त्य: परमक्रुद्धस्ताटकामपि शप्तवान्पुरुषादी महायक्षी विरूपा विकृतानना।इदं रूपं विहायाथ दारुणं रूपमस्तु ते
verses.trans: Mighty angry, Agastya cursed Tataka saying, 'Abandon this form of a great yakshini and assume the terrible figure of a rakshasi, a cannibal with distorted appearance and a hideous countenance'.

words.m: let it be a terrible form.

verses.dev: सैषा शापकृतामर्षा ताटका क्रोधमूर्छिता।देशमुत्सादयत्येनमगस्त्यचरितं शुभम्
verses.trans: Enraged at the curse, senseless, Tataka with anger, has been ravaging this sacred land when Agastya walked.

verses.dev: एनां राघव दुर्वृत्तां यक्षीं परमदारुणाम्।गोब्राह्मणहितार्थाय जहि दुष्टपराक्रमाम्
verses.trans: O Rama for the welfare of cows and brahmins, slay this yakshini who is wicked, extremely cruel and possessing vile prowess.

words.m: for the welfare of brahmins and the cows

verses.dev: न ह्येनां शापसम्स्पृष्टां कश्चिदुत्सहते पुमान्।निहन्तुं त्रिषु लोकेषु त्वामृते रघुनन्दन
verses.trans: O son of Raghu's dynasty she is maligned by the curse, and so not a single man in the three worlds except you, is competent to slay this yakshini.

verses.dev: न हि ते स्त्रीवधकृते घृणा कार्या नरोत्तम।चातुर्वण्यहितार्थाय कर्तव्यं राजसूनुना
verses.trans: O best among men you need not hate killing a woman. You are a prince. You should serve the interest of the four orders of the society.

words.m: by the act of slaying a woman
pity न कार्या need not be shown
in the interests four orders of the society

verses.dev: नृशंसमनृशंसं वा प्रजारक्षणकारणात्।पातकं वा सदोषं वा कर्तव्यं रक्षता सता
verses.trans: Whether cruel or kind, sinful or wrong whatever contributes to the protection of the subjects, should be done by the righteous (king).

words.m: for the welfare of the subjects
or not cruel ,पातकं वा or a sin

verses.dev: राज्यभारनियुक्तानामेष धर्मस्सनातन:।अधर्म्यां जहि काकुत्स्थ धर्मोह्यस्या न विद्यते
verses.trans: This is the eternal law binding those who are appointed to bear the burden of a kingdom. O Kakutstha kill her. She knows no dharma.

words.m: for those who enjoined by the burden of royalty

verses.dev: श्रूयते हि पुरा शक्रो विरोचनसुतां नृप।पृथिवीं हन्तुमिच्छन्तीं मन्थरामभ्यसूदयत्
verses.trans: O Prince It is heard that in the past Indra slew Manthara, Virochana's daughter who intended to destroy the earth.

words.m: in the days of the past

verses.dev: विष्णुनापि पुरा राम भृगुपत्नी दृढव्रता।अनिन्द्रं लोकमिच्छन्ती काव्यमाता निषूदिता
verses.trans: 'O Rama! In olden times, Kavya, the mother of Sukracharya, the wife of sage Bhrigu, was destroyed by Vishnu determined to destroy Indra.'

verses.dev: एतैश्चान्यैश्च बहुभी राजपुत्र महात्मभि:।अधर्मसहिता नार्यो हता: पुरुषसत्तमै:
verses.trans: O Prince by these magnanimous persons and by great men several unrighteous women were killed for the common good". इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डे पञ्चविंशस्सर्ग:।। Thus ends the twentyfifth sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
